introSection: Big Apple was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Dreamland Margate in Margate, Kent, England, UK. It is believed to have operated in at least three different locations around the park.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Having opened in 1978, this was the first Pinfari Big Apple coaster to open in the United Kingdom.

The roller coaster was replaced by the Looping Star in 2001.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: This roller coaster had two trains and a rotating station, which allowed one train to load and unload while another on the main circuit. The second train and rotating station were eventually removed.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Early photograph of the Big Apple

fullText:
Big Apple was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Dreamland Margate in Margate, Kent, England, UK. It is believed to have operated in at least three different locations around the park.

Having opened in 1978, this was the first Pinfari Big Apple coaster to open in the United Kingdom.

The roller coaster was replaced by the Looping Star in 2001.

This roller coaster had two trains and a rotating station, which allowed one train to load and unload while another on the main circuit. The second train and rotating station were eventually removed.

White track and red supports.




 * Early photograph of the Big Apple
